title: Bucket Types, Endpoints, Regions, and S3 Express · Execution Trace: General Directory Bucket
summary: A client hard-codes path-style URLs, assumes global ordering and lifecycle features, and deploys latency-sensitive work without selecting a compatible bucket type or zone. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the general directory bucket increment in bucket-type and endpoint compatibility matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and eviden…
objectives: Compare general-purpose and directory buckets by namespace, endpoint, zone, ordering, throughput, durability scope, versioning, lifecycle, and feature support.
Use S3 strong read-after-write consistency while preserving single-key atomicity and cross-object transaction limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict General Directory Bucket · Theory General Directory Bucket
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 2442 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate general or directory bucket region, zone, endpoint, reactivation, and session rules; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then change the object version and prove stale authority is rejected. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare general-purpose and directory buckets by namespace, endpoint, zone, ordering, throughput, durability scope, versioning, lifecycle, and feature support.
